calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-22-2025 Set For Trial On Nov-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production To Plaintiff?S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Request For Monetary And Issue Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kailyn J. Sharp, Esq., LAW OFFICE OF KAILYN J. SHARP (628) 220-2990 Kailyn@kjsharplaw.com
Attorney for Plaintiff VALERIE GURROLA
TYLER J. SAN JUAN, ESQ. FRASSETTO LAW LLP (510) 817-0466 rjf@frassettolaw.com Attorney for Defendant ROSS STORES, INC.
Noelle Krawiec, CSR# 14255 661-607-5707 noellekrawiec@gmail.com Court Reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF VALERIE GURROLA Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production To Plaintiff?S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two And Request For Monetary And Issue Sanctions

Plaintiff Valerie Gurrola's motion to compel further responses and production to her set two requests for documents and for monetary and issue sanctions is granted in large part. No later than October 23, 2025 defendant Ross Stores, Inc. is required to produce those portions of all versions of its Emergency Procedures Manual and Occupational Safety & Health Program Manual that have been in effect since December 13, 2024 regarding 1) the recording and/or reporting incidents that result in injuries at the Ross Store located at 2300 16th Street in San Francisco (Store) and 2) the preserving evidence pertaining to incidents that result in injuries at the Store. Also no later than October 23, 2025 Ross and/or its counsel must pay Ms. Gurrola $5,160 in monetary sanctions.

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Court Reporter: Noelle Krawiec, CSR# 14255 661-607-5707 noellekrawiec@gmail.com, Reported(301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff'S Counsel Kailyn Sharp
Off Calendar Oct-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kailyn J. Sharp, Esq., LAW OFFICE OF KAILYN J. SHARP (628) 220-2990 Kailyn@kjsharplaw.com
Attorney for Plaintiff VALERIE GURROLA
TYLER J. SAN JUAN, ESQ. FRASSETTO LAW LLP (510) 817-0466 rjf@frassettolaw.com Attorney for Defendant ROSS STORES, INC. (via ZOOM)
Noelle Krawiec, CSR# 14255 661-607-5707 noellekrawiec@gmail.com Court Reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROSS STORES, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff's Counsel Kailyn Sharp.

Defendant Ross Stores, Inc. motion for protective order and for monetary sanctions is denied. No later than October 23, 2025 Ross and/or its counsel must pay Ms. Gurrola $3.450 in monetary sanctions.

Ross has provided no admissible evidence that the Incident Reporting - Job Aid document is privileged and was inadvertently disclosed. Other than inadmissible conclusory statements, Ross provides no evidence of any attorney or insurer involvement with that document. On its face that document discloses nothing that is privileged. Nor does Ross provide any evidence that Cindy or anyone else at the Store was ever told not to provide that document or any similar document to a person claiming to be injured or that the document was inadvertently disclosed by Cindy or anyone else. A reasonable inference from the admissible evidence on this motion is that Ross employees routinely ask persons claiming to be injured to complete a portion of the document and, despite the document containing the statement of a person claiming injury for which Ross may be liable, Ross instructs its employees to destroy the document. This raises a significant and troubling spoliation concern.

Ross' arguments that the Incident Reporting - Job Aid document and Associate Safety Handbook are trade secrets or protectable confidential information borders on the absurd. Ross provides no evidence of any kind that it makes any effort to keep the information contained in these documents confidential or that the information has any commercial value.

Because Ross' motion lacks substantial justification, Ms. Gurrola is entitled to an award of monetary sanctions for the expense of having to oppose the motion. Because the $3,450 sought by Ms. Gurrola is calculated based on a reasonable hourly rate and reasonable number of hours, that amount is awarded.

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported(301/HEK)
